title: Herbal Teas for Ringworm: 10 Natural Remedies
intro_desc: Herbal teas for ringworm are natural remedies made from various herbs that help alleviate symptoms and speed up the healing process of ringworm, a fungal infection causing red, itchy, and circular patches on the skin. The best herbal teas for ringworm include Melaleuca alternifolia, Calendula officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Allium sativum, Cymbopogon citratus, Curcuma longa, Cinchona officinalis, and Sanguinaria canadensis. If left untreated, ringworm can lead to painful blisters, permanent scarring, and emotional distress, negatively impacting daily life, work, and social interactions, making it essential to seek natural remedies like herbal teas to treat this condition effectively.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tea, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural remedy that can help with ringworm. The main active ingredient in tea tree oil is called melaleuca, which has strong antifungal properties. This means it can fight and kill the fungus that causes ringworm. When you apply tea tree oil to the affected area, it helps to reduce the severity of the symptoms, such as itching and redness. The antifungal properties of melaleuca also help to prevent the fungus from spreading to other parts of the body. In addition, tea tree oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. Using Melaleuca alternifolia tea can be a good alternative to traditional medications, as it is a natural and non-toxic way to treat ringworm.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins may be good for ringworm because they have antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help reduce the growth of fungus that causes ringworm.

Polyphenols have been shown to be effective against ringworm because they have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to kill the fungus and bacteria that cause the infection, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further infection.
Quercetin is good for ringworm because it has strong antifungal properties that can help stop the growth of the fungus that causes ringworm, reducing its symptoms and promoting healing.
Tannins are good for ringworm because they have antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing ringworm, thereby reducing symptoms and promoting healing.

remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal teas for ringworm
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with ringworm, first, you need to gather a few fresh or dried leaves of the plant. If using fresh leaves, pick them from the stem, and if dried, use the powder or dried leaves. Next, boil water in a pot and let it cool for a few minutes. Then, add one teaspoon of the Origanum vulgare leaves to a cup and pour the cooled water over it. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth. Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it. The recommended dosage is to drink 2-3 cups a day for 7-10 days. You can add honey or lemon to the tea if you prefer the taste.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is a natural remedy that has been used to treat ringworm for many years. This is because calendula contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to soothe and calm the skin. When applied topically or taken as a tea, calendula can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection. Calendula also has antifungal properties that can help to combat the fungus that causes ringworm, helping to speed up the healing process. Additionally, calendula tea can be used to boost the immune system, which is essential for fighting off infections and promoting overall health. The anti-inflammatory properties in calendula can also help to reduce itching and discomfort associated with ringworm, making it a more comfortable and manageable condition to live with.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for ringworm because it has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help reduce redness, itching, and the growth of the fungus that causes ringworm.
Saponins are good for ringworm because they have antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing the infection and soothe the affected skin.
Kaempferol is good for ringworm because it has strong antifungal properties that help fight and kill the fungus causing the infection, promoting healing and reducing symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for ringworm
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with ringworm, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried oregano leaves. You can grow oregano in your garden or purchase it from a health food store. If using fresh leaves, rinse them with clean water and chop them finely. Next, boil a cup of water and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add 1-2 teaspoons of the chopped oregano leaves to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the tea will be. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the oregano leaves. You can add honey or lemon to taste. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and heal ringworm. Keep in mind that oregano has antifungal properties, which can help combat ringworm, but it's also essential to consult a doctor or a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica tea is a traditional remedy for ringworm. The tea is made from the leaves of the neem tree, a plant that has been used for centuries in Ayurvedic medicine. The active compounds in neem, such as azadirachtin and nimbin, have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to combat the fungus causing ringworm. These compounds can help to reduce the severity of symptoms like itching and redness, and can even prevent the fungus from spreading. The antifungal properties of neem can also help to promote healing and reduce the risk of scarring. Additionally, neem tea is easy to make and has few side effects, making it a popular choice for treating ringworm. When applied topically or consumed as a tea, the compounds in neem can help to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery from ringworm.

Azadirachtin in the Neem tree is good for ringworm because it has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and heal the skin affected by the fungal infection, reducing redness, itching, and the growth of the fungus.
Tannins are good for ringworm because they have antifungal properties that help stop the growth of the fungus that causes ringworm.
Saponins are good for ringworm because they have antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing the infection, thereby reducing symptoms and promoting healing.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with ringworm, you will need a few fresh or dried leaves of the plant. First, if using fresh leaves, pick a few sprigs and rinse them gently with water. Then, place the leaves in a pot and pour in about 2 cups of boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water. If using dried leaves, simply add 1-2 teaspoons to the pot and pour in the boiling water. After the steeping time is over, let the tea cool down for a few minutes before straining it with a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. The tea is now ready to drink. To use it for treating ringworm, apply a few drops of the cooled tea directly to the affected area with a clean cotton swab. Repeat this process a few times a day for best results. It's also essential to note that Origanum vulgare tea may not be a standalone treatment for ringworm and should be used in conjunction with other medical treatments under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a herbal remedy that may help with ringworm. Ringworm is a fungal infection that causes a red, itchy rash. Echinacea purpurea is a plant with natural antifungal properties that can help fight the fungus causing the infection. The tea made from this plant can be applied directly to the affected area to reduce the itching and inflammation. The antifungal properties in Echinacea purpurea tea can also help to prevent the fungus from spreading. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the skin and reduce the discomfort caused by the ringworm. Some people also use Echinacea purpurea tea as a preventative measure to avoid getting ringworm in the first place.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for ringworm because they have antifungal properties that help to stop the growth of fungus that causes the infection, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected skin.
Alkylamides are good for ringworm because they have antifungal properties that can help kill the fungus causing the infection, reducing symptoms and promoting healing.
Quercetin is good for ringworm because it has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help reduce redness, itching, and the growth of fungi causing the infection.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for ringworm
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with ringworm, first gather fresh or dried oregano leaves, which are the source of the herb. You can use either the leaves of Origanum vulgare, also known as wild marjoram, or the related Origanum majorana, also known as sweet marjoram, which are more commonly used for cooking. Next, measure out one teaspoon of dried leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves for every 8 oz of boiling water. Pour the boiling water over the leaves in a cup or teapot, then let it steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water. Finally, strain the tea into another cup and let it cool down before applying it to the affected area with a cotton swab. However, it's essential to note that ringworm can be contagious, so it's crucial to practice good hygiene and keep the affected area clean while using the oregano tea as a complementary treatment to other medical remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Allium sativum, also known as garlic, has been used for many years to treat ringworm. The antifungal properties in garlic help to kill the fungus that causes ringworm, making it a good treatment option. The sulfur compounds in garlic are particularly effective against fungal infections. When made into a tea, the active ingredients in garlic can be absorbed by the skin, helping to reduce the severity and duration of ringworm. Drinking garlic tea has also been shown to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the infection more effectively. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of garlic can help to prevent the spread of ringworm to other parts of the body. To use garlic tea to treat ringworm, you can steep crushed garlic cloves in hot water, then apply the liquid to the affected area with a clean cloth.

Quercetin is good for ringworm because its antifungal properties help reduce the growth of fungi that cause the infection, promoting healing and preventing further spread.
Polyphenols are good for ringworm because they have antifungal properties that help to kill the fungus causing the infection and promote healing of the affected skin.
Kaempferol has been found to be good for ringworm because it possesses antifungal properties that help to reduce the growth of fungal infections and promote healing in affected skin areas.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with ringworm, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: fresh or dried oregano leaves, boiling water, and a clean cup. Begin by measuring out one to two teaspoons of oregano leaves for every cup of water you plan to use, depending on your desired strength of tea. Next, add the measured oregano leaves to a clean cup and pour boiling water over them, making sure the leaves are fully submerged. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled down to a comfortable temperature. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea into another cup to remove the oregano leaves, and discard the solids. Finally, drink the tea three times a day to help soothe and heal the affected area of ringworm, and also consider applying a cooled version of the tea to the affected area with a clean cloth to help speed up the healing process.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea is a natural remedy that can help treat ringworm. The main reason for this is the aloe vera's antifungal properties. Aloe vera contains compounds that can fight off the fungi that cause ringworm, such as Trichophyton and Microsporum. When you drink aloe vera tea, these compounds enter your body and help to reduce the fungus's growth. This makes it easier for your body to fight off the infection and heal the affected area. Additionally, aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm. Drinking aloe vera tea regularly may also boost your immune system, which can help to prevent future infections.

Aloe-emodin has been found to be good for ringworm because it has strong antifungal properties that help to kill the fungus that causes ringworm, thereby reducing symptoms and promoting healing.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with ringworm, you will need some fresh or dried Oregano leaves. Start by washing your hands and a clean bowl, then add one teaspoon of dried Oregano leaves or three to four teaspoons of fresh leaves to a cup of boiling water. Let it steep for five to ten minutes, then strain the tea into another clean cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves. You can add honey to sweeten the tea if needed. Drink the tea three times a day, preferably on an empty stomach, to help treat ringworm. It's also essential to note that you should consult a doctor or a herbalist before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have a severe case of ringworm or if you're taking any medications. Additionally, wash the affected area with soap and water and use a topical antifungal cream to help speed up the healing process.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including skin problems like ringworm. Ringworm is a fungal infection that causes red, itchy patches on the skin. The antifungal properties of ginger tea make it effective in treating ringworm. Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have been shown to have antifungal properties, which can help kill the fungus that causes ringworm. Drinking ginger tea may help to reduce the symptoms of ringworm, such as itching and redness, and can also help to promote healing of the affected skin. Additionally, ginger tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the swelling and redness associated with ringworm. To use ginger tea for ringworm, you can make a tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water, then apply the cooled tea to the affected area with a clean cloth. This can be done several times a day to help promote healing and reduce symptoms.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea to help with ringworm, start by gathering a handful of dried oregano leaves, which can be found in most health food stores or grown at home. You will also need a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Boil a cup of water and pour it over the oregano leaves in the infuser or bag. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their medicinal properties into the water. Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool slightly. You can add a bit of honey to make the tea more palatable, but be sure to consult with a doctor or herbalist before consuming it, especially if you have any underlying health conditions. The cooled tea can be applied directly to the affected area using a cotton ball or swab, or taken internally as a treatment for ringworm. It is essential to note that Origanum vulgare should not be used as the sole treatment for ringworm and should be used in conjunction with other treatments under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a natural remedy that may help with ringworm. The main active ingredient in this tea is eucalyptol, a compound that has antifungal properties. This means it can help kill the fungus that causes ringworm, which is a common skin infection.  Eucalyptol in the tea can also help reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe the skin and make the symptoms more manageable. Additionally, eucalyptol can help promote healing by improving blood flow to the affected area.  Eucalyptus globulus tea has been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including ringworm. Some studies have shown that it can be as effective as conventional treatments, but with fewer side effects. However, it's essential to note that the tea may not work as quickly as other treatments, and it's often used in combination with other natural remedies for better results.  To use eucalyptus globulus tea for ringworm, it's recommended to drink 1-2 cups a day and apply a cooled tea bag to the affected area as a compress. You can also add the tea to your bath water for a relaxing soak. However, always consult with a healthcare professional before using the tea, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for ringworm
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea to help with ringworm, you will need a few simple ingredients. First, find some fresh or dried Oregano leaves, which are the leaves of the Origanum vulgare plant. If you can't find fresh ones, use dried ones instead. Next, boil some water in a pot and then remove it from the heat. Add one teaspoon of the Oregano leaves to the hot water and let it steep for about 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their goodness into the water. Strain the tea using a fine mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth into a cup, and discard the Oregano leaves. Finally, drink the tea two to three times a day, ideally after meals, to help soothe and calm the ringworm. You can also add a bit of honey to the tea to make it taste sweeter.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tea is a natural remedy that may help with ringworm. The main ingredient in this tea, curcumin, has powerful antifungal properties. This means it can fight the fungus that causes ringworm, helping to reduce its symptoms. The fungus that causes ringworm can cause red, itchy, and scaly patches on the skin. Curcumin in Curcuma longa tea can help to reduce inflammation and kill the fungus, which can help to clear up the symptoms of ringworm. The antifungal properties of curcumin may also help to prevent the fungus from spreading to other parts of the body. Additionally, Curcuma longa tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and itching. This can make the skin feel more comfortable and improve the healing process. Drinking Curcuma longa tea may also boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the fungus that causes ringworm.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is not found, it is actually found in Curcuma longa. However, I can provide information on the antifungal properties of Origanum vulgare, specifically its essential oil.  Curcumin is not the correct compound for the relief of ringworm. However, the essential oil of Origanum vulgare contains a bioactive compound called carvacrol which is good for ringworm.

remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal teas for ringworm
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with ringworm, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the oregano plant. If using fresh leaves, chop them finely and dry them in a low-temperature oven or a dehydrator for at least 2 hours to remove excess moisture. For dried leaves, you can skip this step. Next, measure out 1 teaspoon of the dried leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water. Pour the boiling water over the leaves in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it. As a treatment for ringworm, drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, ideally 2-3 times a week. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using this tea as a treatment for ringworm, especially if you have a weakened immune system or are taking any medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare, also known as wild marjoram, has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including ringworm. The tea made from its leaves has antifungal properties that help fight the fungus causing ringworm. Ringworm is a skin infection caused by a fungal parasite, and Origanum vulgare tea can help kill this parasite, reducing the risk of further infection. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the affected skin, reducing redness and itching. Additionally, Origanum vulgare tea has been shown to promote wound healing, which is essential for treating ringworm. By applying the tea to the affected area, you can create a barrier that prevents the fungus from spreading and promotes the healing process.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Carvacrol is good for ringworm because it has antimicrobial properties that help reduce fungal infections and promote healing of the affected skin.
Thymol is good for ringworm because it has strong antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing the infection, reducing symptoms such as itching and redness.
Rosmarinic acid is good for ringworm because it has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help kill the fungus causing the infection, thus reducing symptoms and promoting healing.

remedies.image_alt: Origanum vulgare herbal teas for ringworm
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a ringworm herbal tea with Origanum vulgare, also known as wild marjoram, start by gathering a handful of the herb's leaves and flowers. You can find Origanum vulgare in its natural form in many parts of the world or grow it yourself in a sunny spot. Next, dry the gathered herbs thoroughly by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place or by placing them in a low-temperature oven for a few hours. Once the herbs are completely dry, you can use them to make a tea by steeping 1 teaspoon of dried Origanum vulgare leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid and discard the solids, then let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature before applying it to the affected skin area using a cotton ball. It's essential to note that while Origanum vulgare has been traditionally used to help with ringworm, its effectiveness may vary, and it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before using it as a treatment.

supplementary_best: For a remedy combination to treat ringworm, consider blending the herbal teas of 'Melaleuca alternifolia', 'Curcuma longa', and 'Origanum vulgare'. 'Melaleuca alternifolia' is known for its antifungal properties, which helps combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm. 'Curcuma longa' or turmeric contains curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduces itching, redness, and swelling associated with ringworm. 'Origanum vulgare' or oregano has antifungal and antibacterial properties that helps eliminate the fungal spores and bacteria that infect the skin. Together, these three herbal teas work synergistically to effectively treat ringworm by fighting the infection, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing.
supplementary_avoid: If you have ringworm, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. Ginger tea, for example, can slow down healing. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can irritate the skin and make ringworm worse.  Sage tea is also a problem. It has antibacterial properties that can dry out the skin and make it harder for the infection to heal.  Sassafras tea contains safrole, a chemical that can cause skin irritation and worsen ringworm symptoms.  Tea tree oil, often used in herbal teas, can be too harsh for ringworm. It has antifungal properties, but it can also dry out the skin and cause irritation.  Slippery elm tea may not be the best choice for ringworm either. While it's often used to soothe the digestive system, it can cause skin irritation in some people, which may make ringworm worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel has antifungal properties that can help soothe and calm the skin affected by ringworm. When applied topically, it can reduce inflammation and promote healing. Coconut oil, which contains lauric acid, has antifungal properties that can help fight ringworm-causing fungus. It can be applied directly to the affected area, and some people also use it as a hair treatment for scalp ringworm. Tea tree oil, derived from the leaves of the melaleuca tree, is known for its antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it a popular natural remedy for ringworm. When applied topically, it can help reduce the fungal infection and promote healing. Apple cider vinegar has antifungal properties that can help balance the skin's pH and create an environment that's not favorable for fungal growth. It can be applied directly to the affected area or added to a bath for a soothing soak.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have ringworm may also experience other skin problems. Aloe vera can help with eczema, a condition that causes itchy, dry skin. The gel from aloe vera plants has anti-inflammatory properties that can calm irritated skin and reduce itching.  Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound that has antifungal and antibacterial properties. It can be used to treat skin infections like impetigo, which is a bacterial infection that can cause red sores on the skin.  Tea tree oil has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help treat fungal infections like athlete's foot, which is a condition that causes itchy, cracked skin on the feet. It can also be used to treat acne, a skin condition that causes red, painful pimples.  Neem oil has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help treat skin infections like dermatitis, a condition that causes itchy, red skin. It can also be used to treat lice and scabies, which are parasitic infections that can cause itchy skin and red bumps.  Slippery elm can help with skin irritations like diaper rash, which is a condition that causes red, itchy skin on babies. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated skin and reduce itching.
